complaint_what_happened:

complaint_what_happened:
The mortgage company is claiming that they have denied my request to close the escrow account that the only reason they opened was to pay for the hazard insurance. Now they are claiming that they have to keep it open because I supposedly have paid my loan over 30 days for a few months which is false information. This company keeps lying over and over and keeps bringing up the hazard insurance they obtained which I am not disputing. Yes they got the hazard insurance and opened the escrow account for that purpose. The hazard insurance was for {$3900.00} so why are they asking me to pay {$840.00} every month to cover that amount for the next 12 months when that only equals to XXXX XXXX monthly. They are trying to rip me off and continue to charge me for an escrow account that has now been paid in full and has a positive balance. This escrow account should no longer be open because the reason it was opened for has been resolved. The mortgage lender opened this escrow account for the sole purpose of paying the insurance and that has been covered so why would you keep it open now when I am not supposed to have an escrow account? I have attached my payment history showing that the months they are claiming I paid over 30 days late, I actually paid on time and by the end of the month which they advised me is the latest I could before being considered deliqnuent. This mortgage company is a huge fraud and a huge red flag because now they are making up lies to try to continue to charge fraudulently for an escrow account that should be closed. Please find attached the payments made for XXXX, XXXX, XXXX and XXXX all within the month and never once 30 days past due. 

XXXX was paid on XX/XX/XXXX XXXX was paid on XX/XX/XXXX XXXX was paid on XX/XX/XXXX XXXX was paid XX/XX/XXXX XXXX was paid XX/XX/XXXX XXXX was paid XX/XX/XXXX All within the month the payment was due each month and I was told by them that as long as I pay within that month the payment is due, it is not 30 days late and neither of these payments are 30 days late. Now they are lying to say I'm delinquent several times to keep this fraudulent escrow account open and for what? I don't owe you anything anymore. The money that was disbursed on my behalf for the insurance has been paid in full so why would I continue to pay into an escrow account that I am not supposed to have. If I need to obtain a homeowners insurance policy now to cancel this escrow then I need to be told that but no one ever gives me accurate information when I call. They don't know what lies to make up. I was holding off on getting insurance because this insurance is good until XXXX and has been paid off in full so why would I get another insurance policy until then. If I do get an insurance policy effective in XXXX, are they going to refund me for the amount that they were overpaid for and close out the escrow and pay the funds that are sitting in escrow as that were overpaid? This company needs to be reported to the Federal Trade Commission for fraudulent activities and for lying to keep illegal escrow accounts open after the reason they opened it for has been paid in full. I will not pay for any escrow fees when I am not supposed to have one anymore. I pay my taxes directly and I will obtain a homeowners insurance policy in XXXX. As soon as I send proof, I want a refund for everything that they have been overpaid for and the escrow account shut down and my monthly payment back to normal. I also want this company heavily investigated for their fraudulent activities that have been done to my account. 

I'm also attaching the payment confirmation emails for the months they are claiming that I was over 30 days late. I don't have XXXX because I made the payment over the phone since they didn't allow to pay online anymore due to it being grayed out.

I hope this letter finds you well. I am writing to bring to your attention a billing error that I recently discovered in my account with XXXX XXXX This error is in violation of several consumer protection laws, including [ 12 CFR 1026.13 ( A ) ( 1 ), 12 CFR 1002, 1002.2, 16 CFR 681.1, and 15 USC 1601 ]. 

Upon reviewing my consumer report, I noticed a transaction receipt dated XX/XX/2023, indicating a mortgage transaction that was conducted without my authority or knowledge. I have not benefited from this transaction in any way, and it should not have appeared on my account. 

Given the circumstances, I request that you rectify this billing error promptly and take the necessary steps to remove the unauthorized transaction from my account. I also remind you that under the provisions of 15 USC 1640, 15 USC 1693 ( N ), and 15 USC 1691 ( D ) ( 6 ), XXXX XXXX may be held liable for your failure to comply with legal requirements. 

I kindly request that you investigate this matter thoroughly and provide me with a detailed explanation of how this billing error occurred. Additionally, please inform me of the actions you plan to take to rectify the situation and prevent similar incidents from happening in the future. 

If necessary, I am prepared to take legal action to protect my rights and seek compensation for any damages incurred due to this billing error. However, I hope we can resolve this matter amicably and avoid any further complications. 

I look forward to receiving your prompt response within 5 days of the receipt of this letter. 
Thank you for your attention to this matter and your cooperation.
issue: Applying for a mortgage or refinancing an existing mortgage

complaint_what_happened:
Subject : Billing Error Notice and Civil Liability Claim Dear Navy Federal Credit Union, I sincerely hope this letter finds you well. I am writing to inform you about a billing error that I have recently identified in my Navy Federal Credit Union account. It has come to my attention that your institution has failed to comply with specific legal requirements, including [ 12 CFR 1026.13 ( A ) ( 1 ), 12 CFR 1002, 1002.2, 16 CFR 681.1, and 15 USC 1601 ]. As a result, I firmly believe that Navy Federal Credit Union is civilly liable to me under 15 USC 1640, 15 USC 1693 ( N ), and 15 USC 1691 ( D ) ( 6 ).

Upon reviewing my consumer report, I have discovered a mortgage transaction receipt dated XX/XX/XXXX which indicates that a transaction has taken place without my authority or knowledge. To the best of my understanding, I have not received any benefit from this transaction. Such actions are a clear violation of the aforementioned regulations and safeguards designed to protect consumers like myself. 

I kindly request that Navy Federal Credit Union undertakes a thorough investigation into this matter promptly. It is imperative to rectify this billing error as a matter of urgency. I am providing you with this formal notice, as required by law, explicitly referencing the violations of [ 12 CFR 1026.13 ( A ) ( 1 ), 12 CFR 1002, 1002.2, 16 CFR 681.1, and 15 USC 1601 ]. 

Attached herewith is supporting documentation that serves to highlight the discrepancy in my mortgage transaction. Additionally, I have outlined the specific provisions of the laws and regulations that Navy Federal Credit Union appears to have violated. 

I trust that Navy Federal Credit Union will treat this matter with the utmost importance and take immediate action to rectify the error. Please be reminded of the potential penalties that may be imposed upon creditors for non-compliance with their legal obligations under 15 USC 1640, 15 USC 1693 ( N ), and 15 USC 1691 ( D ) ( XXXX ). 

In accordance with the provisions of the aforementioned regulations, I intend to assert my rights and seek adequate compensation for any damages resulting from this violation. Therefore, I kindly request that Navy Federal Credit Union promptly address this issue and provide me with appropriate compensation for the inconvenience and potential harm caused to me and my public reputation. 

I genuinely value the importance of cooperative resolution and hope that Navy Federal Credit Union shares the same commitment to ethical and legal practices. I kindly request your immediate attention to this billing error notice and civil liability claim. 

Within 5 days, please provide an acknowledgment of receipt of this letter and keep me informed of any progress made towards resolving this issue. 

Thank you for your swift attention to this matter. I trust that Navy Federal Credit Union will make a diligent effort to achieve a satisfactory resolution.

complaint_what_happened:
Planethome lending did not pay my escrowed taxes on time and forced me to pay the late fees of {$200.00}. 

As an XXXX XXXX military member living XXXX  I have XXXX rental property back in the United States. I was previously with XXXX XXXX after I realized my mortgage was sold to Planethome lending around XXXX XXXX XXXX 

Around XXXX XXXX XXXX  I get a mailed notice from my local county tax office saying the taxes are delinquent, the deadline is XX/XX/XXXX or my house will go into tax sale. I call Planethome tax department and they inform me the taxes were paid. Planethome lending paid the first installment not the second installment. They informed me to send in documentation on their online web portal to justify my case. Well they say a formal response will be issued with in XXXX  days. At that time I did not have XXXX  days to wait around and maybe get the correct response so I paid the bill and excess myself to prevent any litigation or my home going into tax sale. Low and behold Planethome lending paid the same bill minus the late fees a couple of weeks later.

After uploading all documentation on multiple occasions and emailing it to their customer service, today I get a letter saying my tax bill was revised to due an exemption and therefore is the customers responsibility, they are unable to refund the late fees/penalty.

1. First off my exemption status for the year XXXX did not change. Illinois pays property taxes for the previous year. 
2. All the tax bills from the entire county were due at the same dates. Planet home lending paid the bill late.

3. I have rented out homes for XXXX years using mortgage companies and escrow and never once have I paid the taxes out of my pocket.

4. If the taxes were my responsibility why did Planethome make any of the payments at all?

5. If planethome was not going to pay my taxes they should of communicated this to me beforehand and not collected taxes in my mortgage payment. Couldn't they have communicated to me prior to this happening?

complaint_what_happened:
I have a mortgage loan from Freedom Mortgage. I received an unexpected call from Freedom Mortgage XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX. XXXX XXXX. He had advised that Freedom Mortgage was the proactively offering me a refinance offer due lower rates with FHA and that it wouldnt cost me anything, and with no closing costs. I told him this all sounds great but it sounds too good to be true. He said they were offering this so I wouldnt refinance anywhere else. That night I went through all of the paperwork and signed the okay. Two-three days later I receive a document requesting that I sign an agreement approve an increased loan amount. I did not sign as it was a redflag to me. After trying to get a hold of XXXX XXXX via email and my credit personal loan phone for a few days, he finally answers from my work number. I address my concern and he tells me that at the time of closing, the numbers will make sense again. The closing documents will reflect what he had told me in the original call. He tells me I dont have to sign this new document. I hang up and decide just to play it by ear. A few days pass again and I get a notification that its time for closing and I receive another document ( estimated closing cost ). Again nothing matches what he says. After several more emails and calls go unanswered, XXXX XXXX calls me back. His name is XXXX XXXX. XXXX acknowledges that he reviewed the calls that I had with XXXX and that he had misinformed me but it was just a simple oversight. I point out that XXXX had multiple opportunities to tell me he was misleading me ( point blank saying it sounded to good to be true/whats the catch?, follow up call asking why the document is now including closing costs ). XXXX informs me that my refinance will have closings costs and asks if I want to continue. I tell him no and that there is no way what XXXX did was a simple oversight. He had many opportunities to try and tell me he made a mistake. 

My concern is that I luckily caught it in time. How many other times has XXXX/Freedom Mortgage done this and gotten away with it? These are just predatory practices. Especially given that if you got a loan in the past year, your rates are real high and most lenders people want to improve it. If I hadnt caught it, I would have got a better rate but when rates really do lower, I would have a hard to refinancing again due to the closing costs at the expense of my equity. How many people have been scammed into this? Please look into this. I am so disgusted with Freedom Mortgage.
issue: Applying for a mortgage or refinancing an existing mortgage

complaint_what_happened: Despite an overage to my escrow account balance my mortgage company insists that my monthly mortgage payment must be increased to satisfy annual payments for mortgage insurance and property tax. In XXXX my mortgage payment increased from XXXX to XXXX. The mortgage lender, upon my inquiry, explained the aforementioned increase was a result of an increased escrow balance requirement due to increasing mortgage insurance and property tax. In an effort to avoid future negative escrow balance issues, I made an additional surplus payment causing an overage to my escrow balance. This XX/XX/XXXX I was informed by the mortgage lender that my mortgage payment would increase due to an increased escrow balance requirement. Despite my current escrow balance overage, the lender issued a check, to me, for the overage and increased my monthly mortgage payment for the XXXX calendar year to offset the increased escrow balance requirement resulting from increases to my mortgage insurance and property tax. I was able to make contact with the escalation department representative, XXXX, who returned the overage funds to the escrow account at my request, but informed me that this action would have no impact on the pending increased monthly payment ; additionally she informed me that any future increase to my property tax and/or mortgage insurance would cause an increase to the escrow balance requirement resulting in an increase to my monthly mortgage payment throughout the life of the loan.

complaint_what_happened: Flagstar mortgage has my XX/XX/2023 payment listed on my credit report is 30 to 59 days late. Ive contacted them multiple times Ive sent them multiple factors I filled out their credit dispute form. I have a copy of the letter showing that I was approved for the HAF housing assistance program through the government. The government even gave them payments Yet they still reported negatively to my credit. This government program has a stipulation in there that mortgage companies can not report negatively to the credit if a person is approved for this program because sometimes HAF takes a while to actually distribute the payment. They did get the payment, but they reported to my credit and they are refusing to remove that late payment off of there. It says the servicer must waive all late charges, fees and penalties and may not report it to the credit report. Im not working right now and theyre negative report lowered my credit score which is causing credit companies to reduce my credit limit its messed up.

On XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, in the amount {$770.00} to Navy Federal. See attached. 

Navy Federal acknowledged receipt of the check on XX/XX/2023, with a notation " XXXX XX/XX/2023 XXXX '' on the reverse side of the check. 

Notwithstanding Navy Federal 's possession of the check on XX/XX/XXXX, a late fee of {$31.00} was assessed. Navy Federal processed the check for payment on XX/XX/2023 as noted on the back of the check. Funds were withdrawn from the XXXX bank account on XX/XX/2023. 

On XX/XX/2023, I contacted Navy Federal and requested the charge be immediately reversed as erroneous and was told I had to wait at least ten days for an investigation to be completed. However, as the notation on the back of the check made clear, there is nothing to investigate since Navy Federal 's own records/notation acknowledged possession of the check on XX/XX/2023 ( within the time period to be considered on time per Navy Federal 's XXXX ). Also, Navy Federal refused to provide/confirm this information currently within their holdings. Lastly, I believe Navy Federal systemically abuses late fee charges in contravention of internal policy because I was told a prior payment late fee had been waived ( without my knowledge that Navy Federal even considered the payment late ).

complaint_what_happened:
*Mortgage Assistance Paperwork sent to Carrington on XX/XX/2023 XXXXOn XX/XX/2023, a Reinstatement Letter was emailed to our email with the amount needed to reinstate the loan. The amount was {$8000.00} to be received by Carrington on XX/XX/2023 at XXXX XXXX  Pacific Standard Time. 

*Received a voicemail from XXXX XXXX on XX/XX/2023 at XXXX XXXX  *requesting a return call. 
*Returned phone call to Carrington at XXXX XXXX  and spoke to a female representative. 
Confirmed with representative that there would be no issues wiring the money on Saturday XX/XX/2023 from XXXX XXXX. Representative assured us that there would be no problems as long as the wire had an origination date on or before XX/XX/XXXX and the wire would be processed on Tuesday, XX/XX/2023. I confirmed again that there would be no issues due to the XXXX XXXX holiday, she reiterated what she had previously told me. 

XXXXOn XX/XX/2023 a wire transfer was sent from XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX MI at exactly XXXX XXXX  in the amount of {$8000.00} from an account belonging to XXXX XXXX XXXX 

*The transfer was initiated by XXXX XXXX and approved by XXXX XXXX who was shown the instructions detailed in the Reinstatement Letter sent by Carrington Mortgage .

*On XX/XX/2023 at XXXX XXXX  we contacted XXXX  and spoke to XXXX XXXXo make sure the payment was received. XXXX said it had not yet been processed and to call back later in the afternoon as there can be a delay after a holiday. 

XXXXOn XX/XX/2023 called Carrington at XXXX XXXX  to check on the status of the payment. Carrington was still unable to locate the payment, we were asked to call back. 

XXXX XXXX, XXXX  called Carrington at XXXX XXXX  to inquire about the status of the payment. Call was escalated to a supervisor who still could not locate payment. Was told if the payment was bounced back to the account, the sale of the house would stand. 

XXXX XXXX, XXXX at XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX received a text from XXXX who informed us he had purchased our property at auction. 

XXXX XXXX XXXX  at XXXX XXXX XXXX called Carrington again to locate the funds. XXXX was instructed to email XXXX XXXX with the wire transfer paperwork, which included the exact amount ( {$8.00}, XXXX ) and the transaction number ( XXXX ) and was told the cashier department would be able to locate the payment using both the amount and transaction number. 

*Called Carrington again on XX/XX/2023 at XXXX XXXX  to see if the payment had been located. Was told that the cashier department was closed for the evening and to call back in the morning. 

*Several calls were made on XX/XX/2023 at XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. Each time we spoke to a different representative. Each time we were asked to give more time and call back and that the situation was escalated to a supervisor. 

*The money was pushed back to XXXX XXXX account XXXX XXXX  from Carrington Mortgage company. This was after spending the entire day on the phone with Carrington and being told they did not have the money, yet Carrington pushed the money back to the account. It was not returned for insufficient funds.

We never received a notice from the sheriff that our house was foreclosed. There was no notice of sale posted. We never received a certified letter saying the house was foreclosed. We have paperwork showing we made the payment prior to the date stated in the Reinstatement Letter. That paperwork is date and time stamped. We provided a very unique payment amount, as well as a transaction number that were both searchable according to Carringtons own representatives. Carrington contends they never had the money yet Carrington returned the money to XXXX XXXX account. I am surprised that they can return money that was not in their possession. The home was sold Tuesday morning before Carrington would have the appropriate time to process the payments made over the weekend, which was confirmed by Carringtons own representatives when they continued to tell us to call back that the payment takes time to process.

We believe that Carrington intentionally did not apply the payment. Carrington stood to make more money with the sale of our property than to apply the payment to our loan so we could resume payments. Carrington failed to properly notify us of the foreclosure and impending sale as required by law. At the very least, this is a terribly unethical practice which is par for the course for Carrington. It seems that the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau has taken action against Carrington for previous deceptive practices.

I have no missed calls from them and no messages on my online account or on my phone. It seems like they are purposely not trying to work with me because I can afford to pay it back. The application is ridiculously long and difficult and they keep saying to do it all again. I even had a manger tell me I was approved and then never would call me back and no one can find that anywhere now. They only want to talk about foreclosure options or refinancing with them. They also blame the VA and their rules that are the issue. That there would not be a reason for refinancing unless I defaulted, after the agreed on repayment plan. I am at a loss and do not know what to do I am a XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX veteran and dont want to lose my house or my 2.25 % APR. Please help!
